You Are the Heart of Our Care Ecosystem
What you can expect as a full-time Care Professional at Homage
Meaningful Work
Make a real difference in the lives of individuals and families — supporting daily living, recovery, and overall well-being.
Structured Onboarding & Training
All CPs complete onboarding, training, and assessments before deployment — ensuring safety, quality, and confidence.
Professional Standards & Accountability
Adhere to Homage's care protocols, code of conduct, and documentation practices. Punctuality and professionalism are core to trust.
Operational Support
The Care Operations team supports scheduling, coordination, and issue escalation. You are never alone on the ground.
Continuous Improvement
Ongoing feedback, performance reviews, and training opportunities to help you grow professionally throughout your journey.

4
Care in Singapore
Government support helps families access the care you provide
🏠
Home Care
Nursing, therapy, and personal care delivered at the client’s home. This is where most of your work happens.
Up to 80%
subsidy for eligible families (from 2026)
🏛
Day & Residential Care
Senior activity centres, rehab centres, and nursing homes. Homage partners with many of these organisations.
100+
partner organisations across Singapore
💲
Additional Support
CareShield Life payouts, caregiver grants, and equipment subsidies help families afford long-term care.
500K+
care hours delivered by Homage per year
©2026 Homage Co Pte Ltd. All rights reserved. Confidential & for internal use only.
5
How Homage Works
Four stakeholders in our care model
👴
Care Recipient (CR)
The elderly person or patient receiving care at home. They are the reason we exist.
👪
Care Owner / Next of Kin
The family member who arranges and coordinates care for their loved one.
💪
Care Professional (CP) THIS IS YOU
You deliver care directly. You are the face of Homage on the ground — your quality of care shapes every family’s experience.
🏧
Partner Organisations
Hospitals, nursing homes, and VWOs who refer patients to Homage for home care support.
©2026 Homage Co Pte Ltd. All rights reserved. Confidential & for internal use only.
7
What You’ll Be Doing
Two types of visits — your care plan specifies exactly what applies
🛌
Personal Care & Hygiene
Bathing, grooming, continence aids, foot care, and skin hygiene around catheters.
👤
Toileting
Assist with commode, bedpan, or diaper changes. Maintain hygiene and dignity at all times.
🤜
Companionship & Staying Active
Light conversation, client-appropriate activities, and prescribed mobility exercises.
🍱
Meals & Nutrition
Buy or prepare meals, follow dietary needs (e.g. diabetic, soft diet), assist with oral feeding.
💊
Medication Reminders
Remind client to take medication on schedule. Notify family and Homage for any missed doses.
🏠
Everyday Housekeeping
Client’s bedroom, bathroom, kitchen. Laundry up to 10 pieces. No heavy-duty cleaning.
🧹
Module A — Housekeeping Visits
Structured cleaning visits: sweep, mop, heavy housekeeping, window cleaning, laundry. Separate from care visits — your roster will specify when these apply.
©2026 Homage Co Pte Ltd. All rights reserved. Confidential & for internal use only.
7
Our Shared Commitment

By joining Homage, you are committing to uphold our standards of care, professionalism, and integrity. In return, we are committed to providing you with the support, structure, and environment needed for you to perform your role effectively and sustainably.

Together, we aim to build a trusted care ecosystem that improves lives — one care interaction at a time.

6
Getting Around Singapore
Singapore has excellent public transport — here is how to use it
🚉
MRT (Train)
Connects most parts of Singapore. Familiarise yourself with the MRT map and the lines near your assignments. Fast, reliable, and air-conditioned.
🚌
Public Bus
Covers areas not served by MRT — often the last leg to a care recipient’s home. Use the SG Buses or Google Maps app to plan bus routes.
🚊
EZ-Link Card
Tap in and out on both MRT and buses. Your card is pre-loaded for onboarding week. Top up at any MRT station or 7-Eleven when needed.
📍
Google Maps
Plan your route the night before every new assignment. Shows real-time MRT and bus timings, walking directions, and estimated travel time.
🍲 Breaks & Meals
Hawker centres and kopitiams: SGD 3–5. Food courts: SGD 5–8. Search “hawker centre near me” in Google Maps between visits. Convenience stores (7-Eleven, Cheers) are also close to most assignments.
©2026 Homage Co Pte Ltd. All rights reserved. Confidential & for internal use only.
6
Your First Week in Singapore
Here is what the next five days look like
Mon
Arrival Day
Arrive at Changi Airport. Medical check-up at clinic. Check in to accommodation.
Day 1
Tue
Aaxonn Training
Full-day Basic Elderminding Course at Aaxonn Training Centre.
Day 2
Wed
Caregiver Training
Full-day Basic Caregiver Training (CGT) at Homage HQ. Lunch with manager.
▶ Day 3 — Today
Thu
CPR/AED + Onboarding
CPR/AED certification. Employment & contract sessions. Module A housekeeping training.
Day 4
Mon
MOM + First Visit
MOM thumbprinting (Day 5). First client visit with joint supervision — employment & payroll starts.
Day 5 + Work Day 1
©2026 Homage Co Pte Ltd. All rights reserved. Confidential & for internal use only.

8
What’s in Your Welcome Kit
Everything you need to start your first week
👕
Homage T-Shirts ×4
Wear one on every visit. Clean, neat, and tucked in. This is your uniform.
🧤
Gloves & Apron
Mandatory PPE for personal care and showering assistance on every relevant visit.
🚊
EZ-Link Card
Pre-loaded to cover your transport during training & onboarding week. Top up at any MRT station or 7-Eleven thereafter.
📚
Printed Handbook
Your reference guide throughout employment. The digital version is always kept up to date.
🨫
Vital Signs Equipment
Blood Pressure monitor, Thermometer, and Oximeter. Collected at onboarding — bring to every visit where vital signs are required.
🎁
Homage Welcome Perks
A welcome gift to start your journey with us — a tote bag, notebook, mug, and a few surprises from the team.
